title: gratiola linifolia var. broteri
intro: Gratiola linifolia var. broteri, also known as Broter's licorice flower, is a plant species within the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Gratiola linifolia var. broteri include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Portugal and Spain.
